title: Mildly Charred Mexican Corn Elote
description: A traditional Mexican dish featuring charred corn on the cob, steamed in lotus leaf wraps, perfect for a hangover cure or a side dish, made with strong white flour and shojin-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat a grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Grill the corn on the cob for about 10 minutes, turning every 2 minutes, until mildly charred.
In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, cotija cheese, chili powder, paprika, lime juice, salt, and pepper.
Once the corn is charred, remove it from the grill and brush it with the mayonnaise mixture.
Wrap each ear of corn in a lotus leaf wrap, making sure to seal the edges.
Steam the wrapped corn for about 5 minutes, until the corn is tender and the leaf is fragrant.
Serve the elote hot, with additional mayonnaise and cotija cheese on the side if desired.
Garnish with fresh cilantro or scallions, if desired.
